symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrical smooth poorly-defined facial brown patches on sun-exposed areas

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetrical smooth macular facial pigmentation with UV/hormonal triggers is characteristic of melasma.
Melanoma is asymmetric/irregular/often single, not symmetrical smooth patches.
Absence of a focal atypical lesion lowers melanoma probability.
Normal BP and no systemic/mucosal pigmentation argue against adrenal insufficiency.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Clinical diagnosis; dermatology/dermoscopy only if a focal lesion appears
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Melasma is clinical; investigate only atypical lesions.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Adrenal work-up only if systemic features suggest Addison's
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated here given no systemic hyperpigmentation/features.
